# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

 - Replace serde_json::Value fields with typed structs or custom Opaque newtype [23]
 - Add error test data and library tests for known-bad inputs [24]
 - Improve error output format (columnization, full paths) [25]
 - Refactor common metadata fields into a shared SessionMetadata struct [4]
 - Have claude code design claude-code a session viewer [1]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

 - Define serde structs for JSONL deserialization (0.1.0) [2]

 - Add support for agent session files [12]
 - Add agent metajson support 0.10.0 [13]
 - Add agentid to record structs for agent jsonl 0.11.0 [14]
 - Add new optional fields 0.12.0-dev1 [15]

471:## Replace serde_json::Value with typed structs
472-
473-  Several struct fields use `serde_json::Value` as a catch-all for
474-  JSON we haven't fully typed yet. This works but loses type safety
475-  and doesn't catch schema changes via `deny_unknown_fields`.
476-
477-  Fields currently using Value:
478-  - `UserRecord`: thinkingMetadata, toolUseResult, todos
479-  - `SystemRecord`: error, compactMetadata
480-  - `ProgressData`: message, prompt, normalizedMessages,
481-    serverToolUse, iterations
482-  - `QueueOperationRecord`: content
483-  - `AssistantRecord` nested: serverToolUse, iterations,
484-    caller, container, contextManagement
485-
486-  Options:
487-  1. Typed structs per field — best safety, most work, risks
488-     breakage on upstream schema changes
489-  2. Custom `Opaque(Value)` newtype — signals "structured but
490-     not yet typed", distinguishes from deliberately modeled
491-     fields, can incrementally promote to real structs
492-
493-  If we go with a catch-all, prefer our own type over bare
494-  `serde_json::Value` so grep can find all untyped fields and
495-  we can add validation or logging in one place later.
496-
497-## Add error test data and library tests
498-
499-  Currently all test data in `data/` is valid. We need
500-  intentionally-bad test data to verify error handling:
501-
502-  - `data/errs/` directory with small files that trigger each
503-    error category (unknown variant, bad field type, etc.)
504-  - Library tests that assert `is_err()` on known-bad data
505-  - Prevents accidental "fixing" of errors we expect to reject
506-
507-  Prefer library tests (in `types::tests`) over integration
508-  tests — the deser behavior is all in `lib.rs` and library
509-  tests run with `cargo test` alongside existing tests.
510-
511-## Improve error output format (columnization, full paths)

content.text:
Here's the summary of the first todo item:

**Replace `serde_json::Value` with typed structs [23]** (current version: 0.12.1)

There are ~17 fields across the codebase still using bare `Value`:

| Struct | Fields |
|---|---|
| `UserRecord` | `thinking_metadata`, `tool_use_result`, `todos`, `tracked_file_backups` |
| `SystemRecord` | `error`, `compact_metadata` |
| `ProgressData` | `message`, `prompt`, `normalized_messages`, `server_tool_use`, `iterations` |
| `QueueOperationRecord` | `content` |
| `AssistantRecord` / nested | `server_tool_use`, `iterations`, `container`, `context_management`, `caller` |
| `ToolUse` variant | `input` |
| `ContentBlock::Structured` | inner `Value` |

The chores note outlines two options:
1. **Typed structs per field** — best safety, most work, risks breakage on upstream schema changes
2. **Custom `Opaque(Value)` newtype** — signals "structured but not yet typed", can incrementally promote fields to real structs, easy to grep for remaining untyped fields

Option 2 seems like the pragmatic first step — introduce an `Opaque` newtype wrapper, replace all bare `Value` usages, then incrementally promote individual fields to typed structs as needed.

Want to proceed with this? And should we do it as a single step (just the `Opaque` newtype swap) or multi-step (newtype + type some fields)?

message.content: My thoughts on this is that serde_json::Value isn't under our control all of the other types are either language based types or ones we've created and use language types, hence using serde_json::Value feels weird, any additional thoughts?

content.text:
Good point. `serde_json::Value` is an external library type leaking into your domain model — it's the only non-language, non-project type in your struct fields. An `Opaque(Value)` newtype fixes that: the public API speaks your types, the serde dependency stays internal, and if you ever swapped JSON libraries the change would be localized to `Opaque`'s impl rather than scattered across every struct.

One additional thought: `Opaque` could carry a `Display`/`Debug` impl that truncates large blobs, which bare `Value` doesn't give you. And you could add a method like `Opaque::try_into::<T>()` later to make incremental promotion to typed structs ergonomic without changing the field type.

Want to proceed? Single-step (introduce `Opaque`, swap all `Value` fields) seems clean here — the change is mechanical. What version do you want to target?

message.content: Is there an Opaque keyword in rust, I looked it apparently this, `type Foo = impl Bar;` is an Opaque type is that what you mean?

content.text:
No, I'm not talking about Rust's opaque type aliases (`type Foo = impl Bar;`, which is still unstable anyway). I'm suggesting we create our own struct literally named `Opaque`:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Opaque(serde_json::Value);
```

It's just a newtype wrapper — a single-field tuple struct. The name `Opaque` is arbitrary, chosen to signal "this is structured data we haven't fully typed yet." We could call it anything: `Untyped`, `RawJson`, `JsonBlob`, etc.

The point is just to wrap `serde_json::Value` so it doesn't appear directly in field signatures. All your struct fields would say `Opaque` or `Option<Opaque>` instead of `Value` or `Option<Value>`.

Does `Opaque` feel like the right name, or would you prefer something else?

message.content:
That feels fine but why do we have to wrap serde_json::Value. I think I need to understand how we will use Opaque(Value). First it appears, Value is an enum that defines several subtypes, Null, Bool, ... from here, https://docs.rs/serde_json/latest/serde_json/value/enum.Value.html. The first use I see in type.rs is:
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
#[serde(deny_unknown_fields, rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct Snapshot {
    pub message_id: String,
    pub tracked_file_backups: Value,
    pub timestamp: String,
}

We should probably start with showing me some instances of the jsonl "FileHistorySnapshotRecord", that might help me.

{"parentUuid":"940a737e-c3aa-4b02-aa42-d3a7bbcad4d9","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01STYVTNfHERqTeJFzEo3YZr","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019veukTz5vf54KD2b1Em1pk","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/wink/.claude/plans/sleepy-toasting-puppy.md","content":"# Plan: Define Serde Structs for JSONL Deserialization (0.1.0)

## Context

First code for ccs-viewer-by-cc. The goal is to create Rust serde structs
that can deserialize every line of a Claude Code session JSONL file.
Sample data: `hwr.claude/31ba272b-f7c2-436b-a017-269e27a64d07.jsonl` (22 lines).

Task: todo.md item [2] / chores-01.md "Define serde structs for JSONL deserialization"

## Version: 0.1.0, single-step

No Cargo.toml exists yet. This is a focused, mechanical task — derive structs
from the JSON schema. **Recommend single-step** (one commit).

## JSONL Record Types (from sample analysis)

| # | `type` field | Description | Lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `file-history-snapshot` | File backup snapshots at conversation start | 1 |
| 2 | `user` | User prompts + tool results | 2,6,9,12,14,17,20 |
| 3 | `assistant` | Model responses (thinking/text/tool_use) | 3,4,5,7,10,13,15,18,21 |
| 4 | `progress` | Hook execution progress events | 8,11,16,19 |
| 5 | `last-prompt` | Session end marker | 22 |

## Struct Design

### Top-level: `Record` — internally tagged enum on `"type"`

```
Record
  ├── FileHistorySnapshot { message_id, snapshot, is_snapshot_update }
  ├── User { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, prompt_id, message: UserMessage, uuid, timestamp, ... }
  ├── Assistant { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, message: AssistantMessage, request_id, uuid, timestamp, ... }
  ├── Progress { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, data: ProgressData, parent_tool_use_id, tool_use_id, ... }
  └── LastPrompt { last_prompt, session_id }
```

### Key sub-structs

**UserMessage** — `{ role, content: UserContent }`
- `UserContent` (untagged enum): `Text(String)` | `Blocks(Vec<ToolResultBlock>)`
- `ToolResultBlock` — `{ tool_use_id, content, is_error }`

**AssistantMessage** — `{ model, id, type, role, content: Vec<AssistantContentBlock>, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage }`
- `AssistantContentBlock` (tagged on `"type"`):
  - `Thinking { thinking, signature }`
  - `Text { text }`
  - `ToolUse { id, name, input: Value, caller }`

**ToolUseResultData** — `serde_json::Value` (too polymorphic for v0.1.0;
4 distinct shapes: string, bash-result, file-read-result, glob-result)

**Usage** — `{ input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation, service_tier, ... }`

**ProgressData** — `{ type, hook_event, hook_name, command }`

**Snapshot** — `{ message_id, tracked_file_backups: Value, timestamp }`

### Design decisions

1. **`serde_json::Value`** for `tool_use.input` and `toolUseResult` — these vary
   per tool type. Typing them fully is a later task.
2. **`Option<T>`** for fields that appear only on some variants of a record type
   (e.g., `slug` appears after first tool use, `permission_mode` only on initial prompt).
3. **`#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`** on structs since the JSON uses camelCase.
4. **Single `types.rs` file** — all structs in one place for now; split when it grows.

## Files to create

```
Cargo.toml            — name: ccs-viewer, deps: serde, serde_json, edition 2024
src/main.rs           — read JSONL path from args, deserialize each line, print summary
src/types.rs          — Record enum + all sub-structs
```

## Verification

1. `cargo fmt` — clean formatting
2. `cargo clippy` — no warnings
3. `cargo test` — unit test: deserialize all 22 lines of sample JSONL
4. `cargo run -- hwr.claude/31ba272b-*.jsonl` — prints record count by type
5. `cargo install --path .` — installs binary
6. Re-run on sample data after install

## Notes updates (at commit time)

- `notes/todo.md` — move item [2] to Done

## Version: 0.1.0, single-step

No Cargo.toml exists yet. This is a focused, mechanical task — derive structs
from the JSON schema. **Recommend single-step** (one commit).

## JSONL Record Types (from sample analysis)

| # | `type` field | Description | Lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `file-history-snapshot` | File backup snapshots at conversation start | 1 |
| 2 | `user` | User prompts + tool results | 2,6,9,12,14,17,20 |
| 3 | `assistant` | Model responses (thinking/text/tool_use) | 3,4,5,7,10,13,15,18,21 |
| 4 | `progress` | Hook execution progress events | 8,11,16,19 |
| 5 | `last-prompt` | Session end marker | 22 |

## Struct Design

### Top-level: `Record` — internally tagged enum on `"type"`

```
Record
  ├── FileHistorySnapshot { message_id, snapshot, is_snapshot_update }
  ├── User { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, prompt_id, message: UserMessage, uuid, timestamp, ... }
  ├── Assistant { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, message: AssistantMessage, request_id, uuid, timestamp, ... }
  ├── Progress { parent_uuid, is_sidechain, data: ProgressData, parent_tool_use_id, tool_use_id, ... }
  └── LastPrompt { last_prompt, session_id }
```

### Key sub-structs

**UserMessage** — `{ role, content: UserContent }`
- `UserContent` (untagged enum): `Text(String)` | `Blocks(Vec<ToolResultBlock>)`
- `ToolResultBlock` — `{ tool_use_id, content, is_error }`

**AssistantMessage** — `{ model, id, type, role, content: Vec<AssistantContentBlock>, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage }`
- `AssistantContentBlock` (tagged on `"type"`):
  - `Thinking { thinking, signature }`
  - `Text { text }`
  - `ToolUse { id, name, input: Value, caller }`

**ToolUseResultData** — `serde_json::Value` (too polymorphic for v0.1.0;
4 distinct shapes: string, bash-result, file-read-result, glob-result)

**Usage** — `{ input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation, service_tier, ... }`

**ProgressData** — `{ type, hook_event, hook_name, command }`

**Snapshot** — `{ message_id, tracked_file_backups: Value, timestamp }`

### Design decisions

1. **`serde_json::Value`** for `tool_use.input` and `toolUseResult` — these vary
   per tool type. Typing them fully is a later task.
2. **`Option<T>`** for fields that appear only on some variants of a record type
   (e.g., `slug` appears after first tool use, `permission_mode` only on initial prompt).
3. **`#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`** on structs since the JSON uses camelCase.
4. **Single `types.rs` file** — all structs in one place for now; split when it grows.

## Files to create

```
Cargo.toml            — name: ccs-viewer, deps: serde, serde_json, edition 2024
src/main.rs           — read JSONL path from args, deserialize each line, print summary
src/types.rs          — Record enum + all sub-structs
```

## Verification

1. `cargo fmt` — clean formatting
2. `cargo clippy` — no warnings
3. `cargo test` — unit test: deserialize all 22 lines of sample JSONL
4. `cargo run -- hwr.claude/31ba272b-*.jsonl` — prints record count by type
5. `cargo install --path .` — installs binary
6. Re-run on sample data after install

- `notes/todo.md` — move item [2] to Done
- `notes/chores-01.md` — add subsection with implementation notes
- `notes/README.md` — add usage section if needed

   per tool type. Typing them fully is a later task.
2. **`Option<T>`** for fields that appear only on some variants of a record type
   (e.g., `slug` appears after first tool use, `permission_mode` only on initial prompt).
3. **`#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`** on structs since the JSON uses camelCase.
4. **Single `types.rs` file** — all structs in one place for now; split when it grows.

## Files to create

```
Cargo.toml            — name: ccs-viewer, deps: serde, serde_json, edition 2024
src/main.rs           — read JSONL path from args, deserialize each line, print summary
src/types.rs          — Record enum + all sub-structs
```

## Verification

1. `cargo fmt` — clean formatting
2. `cargo clippy` — no warnings
3. `cargo test` — unit test: deserialize all 22 lines of sample JSONL
4. `cargo run -- hwr.claude/31ba272b-*.jsonl` — prints record count by type
5. `cargo install --path .` — installs binary
6. Re-run on sample data after install

## Notes updates (at commit time)

- `notes/todo.md` — move item [2] to Done
- `notes/chores-01.md` — add subsection with implementation notes
- `notes/README.md` — add usage section if needed

content.text:
Here's what the data actually looks like:

**Empty case** (most common at session start):
```json
{
  "type": "file-history-snapshot",
  "messageId": "...",
  "snapshot": {
    "messageId": "...",
    "trackedFileBackups": {},
    "timestamp": "2026-03-23T21:46:49.213Z"
  },
  "isSnapshotUpdate": false
}
```

**Non-empty case** — `trackedFileBackups` is a map of filename → backup info:
```json
"trackedFileBackups": {
  "Cargo.toml": {
    "backupFileName": "5e911ea4c8c6aa59@v1",
    "version": 1,
    "backupTime": "2026-03-23T21:53:23.586Z"
  }
}
```

So `tracked_file_backups` is a `HashMap<String, FileBackupEntry>` where `FileBackupEntry` has three fields: `backup_file_name: String`, `version: u64`, `backup_time: String`. That's fully typeable — no need for `Value` or `Opaque` here at all.

This is actually a good example of what the task is about: some of these `Value` fields can be replaced with **real typed structs** once you look at the actual data. Others (like `tool_use.input`, which varies wildly per tool) are genuinely polymorphic and would benefit from the `Opaque` newtype.

Want to start going through each `Value` field this way — look at the actual JSON, decide if it's typeable or needs `Opaque`?

message.content: Ok so some of these are typeable and it seems we should fix those, but if it there are some that are "genuinely polymorphic" then how would be deal with them in the code, I assume each would need a match statement on the subtypes of Value?

content.text: Good question. Let me check how we actually *use* these `Value` fields in the code right now.

content.content: No matches found
377:                    key.message,

content.text:
So — we don't use *any* of those `Value` fields in application logic. They exist purely so `deny_unknown_fields` doesn't reject the records during deserialization. We parse them, store them in the struct, and never look at them.

That changes the picture. For genuinely polymorphic fields, you'd only need `match` on `Value` variants if you were *doing something* with the data (displaying it, filtering on it, transforming it). Right now we're not.

So the practical answer is:

1. **Typeable fields** (like `tracked_file_backups`) → replace with real structs. Better safety, `deny_unknown_fields` catches upstream schema changes at the leaf level.

2. **Polymorphic fields we don't use** (like `tool_use.input`) → `Opaque(Value)` is fine. No match statements needed because we never inspect the contents. It just says "I'm holding structured JSON I don't care about yet." If we ever *do* want to inspect them, that's when we'd either type them fully or add match logic.

The `Opaque` newtype isn't about handling polymorphism differently — it's about labeling intent. `Value` says "this is JSON." `Opaque` says "this is JSON *we chose not to type yet*." When you grep for `Opaque` you see exactly which fields still need work. When there are zero left, the task is done.

Does that clarify things, or do you want to dig into a specific polymorphic field to see what "typing it fully" would look like?

message.content: ATM we are just deserializing and with this explained, I like the idea of "Opaque" being "we chose not to type yet". Maybe UnTyped(Value) or Unknow(Value) ... ?

content.text:
I'd lean toward `Untyped(Value)` — it's the most accurate description: "we know it's valid JSON, we just haven't given it a typed struct yet." 

`Unknown` implies we don't know what it is, but we do — we just haven't bothered to model it. `Untyped` captures that distinction nicely.

Want to proceed with this task? Version 0.13.0, single-step: introduce `Untyped`, replace the typeable fields with real structs, and swap remaining bare `Value` fields to `Untyped`?

# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

CLI usage documentation is in the top-level [README.md](../README.md#cli-usage).

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## jj tips

For users new to jj see [jj-tips.md](jj-tips.md).

```
## Chores format

Filename: "Chores-XX-.md"
example: chores-01.md

Format of section labels: "## <short description> (YYYYMMDD X.Y.Z)"
example: "## Topic format description (20260322 0.1.0)"

Example chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Do something (20260322 1.3.1)

content.content:
1→//! Serde types for Claude Code session JSONL files.
     2→//!
     3→//! Each line of a session file is a JSON object with a `"type"` field that
     4→//! determines its shape. The [`Record`] enum uses serde's internally tagged
     5→//! representation (`#[serde(tag = "type")]`) to dispatch to the correct
     6→//! variant struct.
     7→//!
     8→//! # Key patterns
     9→//!
    10→//! **Label strings** — The `RECORD_LABELS` const is the single source of
    11→//! truth for type name strings (e.g. `"assistant"`, `"system"`).
    12→//! [`Record::label()`] indexes into it; [`Record::all_labels()`] returns
    13→//! the full slice. This avoids duplicating strings between the two methods.
    14→//!
    15→//! **Struct field ordering** — Each struct lists required fields first,
    16→//! then `Option` fields below a separator comment. This makes it visually
    17→//! obvious which fields need entries in the `optional_fields()` list.
    18→//!
    19→//! **`deny_unknown_fields`** — Every struct has `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`
    20→//! so that any JSON key not mapped to a Rust field causes a deserialization
    21→//! error. This ensures we know immediately when Claude Code adds new fields.
    22→//!
    23→//! **`optional_fields()`** — Structs with `Option` fields have a companion
    24→//! `optional_fields()` method listing the camelCase (or snake_case) JSON
    25→//! names of those fields. The `all_optional_fields_seen` test uses these
    26→//! lists to verify every `Option` field is `Some` at least once in test
    27→//! data. Nested fields use dot notation (`"message.usage.speed"`) and
    28→//! array filtering uses brackets (`"message.content[tool_use].caller"`).
    29→//!
    30→//! **Warning**: The `optional_fields()` lists are static — adding an
    31→//! `Option` field to a struct without updating the list means that field
    32→//! silently goes untested. The separator comment in each struct serves as
    33→//! a reminder.
    34→
    35→use serde::Deserialize;
    36→use serde_json::Value;
    37→
    38→/// Top-level record: each line of a Claude Code session JSONL file
    39→/// deserializes into one of these variants, discriminated by the `type` field.
    40→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
    41→#[serde(tag = "type")]
    42→pub enum Record {
    43→    #[serde(rename = "file-history-snapshot")]
    44→    FileHistorySnapshot(FileHistorySnapshotRecord),
    45→
    46→    #[serde(rename = "user")]
    47→    User(Box<UserRecord>),
    48→
    49→    #[serde(rename = "assistant")]
    50→    Assistant(Box<AssistantRecord>),
    51→
    52→    #[serde(rename = "progress")]
    53→    Progress(Box<ProgressRecord>),
    54→
    55→    #[serde(rename = "last-prompt")]
    56→    LastPrompt(LastPromptRecord),
    57→
    58→    #[serde(rename = "queue-operation")]
    59→    QueueOperation(QueueOperationRecord),
    60→
    61→    #[serde(rename = "system")]
    62→    System(Box<SystemRecord>),
    63→
    64→    #[serde(rename = "custom-title")]
    65→    CustomTitle(CustomTitleRecord),
    66→
    67→    #[serde(rename = "agent-name")]
    68→    AgentName(AgentNameRecord),
    69→
    70→    #[serde(rename = "summary")]
    71→    Summary(SummaryRecord),
    72→}
    73→
    74→/// Single source of truth for record type label strings.
    75→const RECORD_LABELS: &[&str] = &[
    76→    "file-history-snapshot",
    77→    "user",
    78→    "assistant",
    79→    "progress",
    80→    "last-prompt",
    81→    "queue-operation",
    82→    "system",
    83→    "custom-title",
    84→    "agent-name",
    85→    "summary",
    86→];
    87→
    88→impl Record {
    89→    /// Returns the type label string for this record variant.
    90→    pub fn label(&self) -> &'static str {
    91→        RECORD_LABELS[match self {
    92→            Record::FileHistorySnapshot(_) => 0,
    93→            Record::User(_) => 1,
    94→            Record::Assistant(_) => 2,
    95→            Record::Progress(_) => 3,
    96→            Record::LastPrompt(_) => 4,
    97→            Record::QueueOperation(_) => 5,
    98→            Record::System(_) => 6,
    99→            Record::CustomTitle(_) => 7,
   100→            Record::AgentName(_) => 8,
   101→            Record::Summary(_) => 9,
   102→        }]
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    /// Returns the full list of known record type labels.
   106→    pub fn all_labels() -> &'static [&'static str] {
   107→        RECORD_LABELS
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    /// Returns the optional fields list for each record type that has them.
   111→    /// Used by tests to verify all Option fields are exercised.
   112→    pub fn optional_fields_by_type() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static [&'static str])> {
   113→        vec![
   114→            ("user", UserRecord::optional_fields()),
   115→            ("assistant", AssistantRecord::optional_fields()),
   116→            ("progress", ProgressRecord::optional_fields()),
   117→            ("queue-operation", QueueOperationRecord::optional_fields()),
   118→            ("system", SystemRecord::optional_fields()),
   119→        ]
   120→    }
   121→}
   122→
   123→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   124→// file-history-snapshot
   125→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   126→
   127→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   128→#[serde(deny_unknown_fields, rename_all = "camelCase")]
   129→pub struct FileHistorySnapshotRecord {
   130→    pub message_id: String,
   131→    pub snapshot: Snapshot,
   132→    pub is_snapshot_update: bool,
   133→}
   134→
   135→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   136→#[serde(deny_unknown_fields, rename_all = "camelCase")]
   137→pub struct Snapshot {
   138→    pub message_id: String,
   139→    pub tracked_file_backups: Value,
   140→    pub timestamp: String,
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   144→// user
   145→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→
   147→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   148→#[serde(deny_unknown_fields, rename_all = "camelCase")]
   149→pub struct UserRecord {
   150→    pub is_sidechain: bool,
   151→    pub message: UserMessage,
   152→    pub uuid: String,
   153→    pub timestamp: String,
   154→    // --- Option fields (add camelCase JSON name to optional_fields below) ---
   155→    pub is_meta: Option<bool>,
   156→    // --- Option fields (add camelCase JSON name to optional_fields below) ---
   157→    pub agent_id: Option<String>,
   158→    pub parent_uuid: Option<String>,
   159→    pub prompt_id: Option<String>,
   160→    pub permission_mode: Option<String>,
   161→    pub tool_use_result: Option<Value>,
   162→    #[serde(rename = "sourceToolAssistantUUID")]
   163→    pub source_tool_assistant_uuid: Option<String>,
   164→    pub user_type: Option<String>,
   165→    pub entrypoint: Option<String>,
   166→    pub cwd: Option<String>,
   167→    pub session_id: Option<String>,
   168→    pub version: Option<String>,
   169→    pub git_branch: Option<String>,
   170→    pub slug: Option<String>,
   171→    pub plan_content: Option<String>,
   172→    pub todos: Option<Value>,
   173→    pub thinking_metadata: Option<Value>,
   174→    pub is_visible_in_transcript_only: Option<bool>,
   175→    pub is_compact_summary: Option<bool>,
   176→}
   177→
   178→// WARNING: When adding an Option field above, add its camelCase JSON name here.
   179→// This list cannot detect missing entries — unlisted fields silently go untested.
   180→impl UserRecord {
   181→    pub fn optional_fields() -> &'static [&'static str] {
   182→        &[
   183→            "agentId",
   184→            "parentUuid",
   185→            "promptId",
   186→            "permissionMode",
   187→            "toolUseResult",
   188→            "isMeta",
   189→            "sourceToolAssistantUUID",
   190→            "userType",
   191→            "entrypoint",
   192→            "cwd",
   193→            "sessionId",
   194→            "version",
   195→            "gitBranch",
   196→            "slug",
   197→            "planContent",
   198→            "todos",
   199→            "thinkingMetadata",
   200→            "isVisibleInTranscriptOnly",
   201→            "isCompactSummary",
   202→            // UserContentBlock::ToolResult
   203→            "message.content[tool_result].is_error",
   204→        ]
   205→    }
   206→}
   207→
   208→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   209→#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]
   210→pub struct UserMessage {
   211→    pub role: String,
   212→    pub content: UserContent,
   213→}
   214→
   215→/// User message content is either a plain text string (initial prompt)
   216→/// or an array of content blocks (tool results, text, etc.).
   217→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   218→#[serde(untagged)]
   219→pub enum UserContent {
   220→    Text(String),
   221→    Blocks(Vec<UserContentBlock>),
   222→}
   223→
   224→/// A block within a user message content array.
   225→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   226→#[serde(tag = "type")]
   227→pub enum UserContentBlock {
   228→    #[serde(rename = "tool_result")]
   229→    ToolResult {
   230→        tool_use_id: String,
   231→        content: ToolResultContent,
   232→        is_error: Option<bool>,
   233→    },
   234→
   235→    #[serde(rename = "text")]
   236→    Text { text: String },
   237→}
   238→
   239→/// Tool result content can be a plain string or a structured value.
   240→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   241→#[serde(untagged)]
   242→pub enum ToolResultContent {
   243→    Text(String),
   244→    Structured(Value),
   245→}
   246→
   247→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   248→// assistant
   249→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   250→
   251→#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
   252→#[serde(deny_unknown_fields, rename_all = "camelCase")]
   253→pub struct AssistantRecord {
   254→    pub is_sidechain: bool,
   255→    pub message: AssistantMessage,
   256→    pub uuid: String,
   257→    pub timestamp: String,
   258→    // --- Option fields (add camelCase JSON name to optional_fields below) ---
   259→    pub parent_uuid: Option<String>,
   260→    pub agent_id: Option<String>,
   261→    pub is_api_error_message: Option<bool>,
   262→    pub request_id: Option<String>,
   263→    pub user_type: Option<String>,
   264→    pub entrypoint: Option<String>,
   265→    pub cwd: Option<String>,
   266→    pub session_id: Option<String>,
   267→    pub version: Option<String>,
   268→    pub git_branch: Option<String>,
   269→    pub slug: Option<String>,
   270→    pub error: Option<String>,
   271→}
   272→
   273→// WARNING: When adding an Option field above (or in nested structs below),
   274→// add its camelCase JSON name here. Nested fields use dot notation.
   275→// This list cannot detect missing entries — unlisted fields silently go untested.
   276→impl AssistantRecord {
   277→    pub fn optional_fields() -> &'static [&'static str] {
   278→        &[
   279→            "parentUuid",
   280→            "agentId",
   281→            "isApiErrorMessage",

   602→#[cfg(test)]
   603→mod tests {
   604→    use super::*;
   605→    use std::collections::HashSet;
   606→    use std::fs::File;
   607→    use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};
   608→
   609→    const TEST_FILES: &[&str] = &[
   610→        "data/31ba272b-f7c2-436b-a017-269e27a64d07.jsonl",
   611→        "data/997afb98-c6aa-4f4a-92ac-a841e040414b.jsonl",
   612→        "data/092de687-cd0d-4583-b872-bc2908dff3ba.jsonl",
   613→        "data/86fb7a89-abfa-4e84-b862-5983e93c0b3b.jsonl",
   614→        "data/ccs-viewer-tests.jsonl",
   615→        "data/agent-test.jsonl",
   616→    ];
   617→
   618→    fn deserialize_file(path: &str) -> usize {
   619→        deserialize_file_collecting(path, None)
   620→    }
   621→
   622→    fn deserialize_file_collecting(path: &str, mut labels: Option<&mut HashSet<String>>) -> usize {
   623→        let file = File::open(path).expect("sample JSONL file should exist");
   624→        let reader = BufReader::new(file);
   625→        let mut count = 0;
   626→        for (i, line) in reader.lines().enumerate() {
   627→            let line = line.unwrap();
   628→            if line.trim().is_empty() {
   629→                continue;
   630→            }
   631→            let result = serde_json::from_str::<Record>(&line);
   632→            assert!(
   633→                result.is_ok(),
   634→                "line {} failed: {}",
   635→                i + 1,
   636→                result.unwrap_err()
   637→            );
   638→            if let (Ok(record), Some(labels)) = (&result, labels.as_deref_mut()) {
   639→                labels.insert(record.label().to_string());
   640→            }
   641→            count += 1;
   642→        }
   643→        count
   644→    }
   645→
   646→    #[test]
   647→    fn deserialize_sample_31ba() {
   648→        assert_eq!(
   649→            deserialize_file("data/31ba272b-f7c2-436b-a017-269e27a64d07.jsonl"),
   650→            22
   651→        );
   652→    }
   653→
   654→    #[test]
   655→    fn deserialize_sample_997a() {
   656→        assert_eq!(
   657→            deserialize_file("data/997afb98-c6aa-4f4a-92ac-a841e040414b.jsonl"),
   658→            461
   659→        );
   660→    }
   661→
   662→    #[test]
   663→    fn deserialize_sample_092d() {
   664→        assert_eq!(
   665→            deserialize_file("data/092de687-cd0d-4583-b872-bc2908dff3ba.jsonl"),
   666→            224
   667→        );
   668→    }
   669→
   670→    #[test]
   671→    fn deserialize_sample_86fb() {
   672→        assert_eq!(
   673→            deserialize_file("data/86fb7a89-abfa-4e84-b862-5983e93c0b3b.jsonl"),
   674→            301
   675→        );
   676→    }
   677→
   678→    /// Look up a dotted path in a serde_json::Value.
   679→    /// Supports segments like "message", "usage", or array filtering
   680→    /// like "content[tool_use]" (find element in array where type == "tool_use").
   681→    fn value_at_path<'a>(val: &'a Value, path: &str) -> Option<&'a Value> {
   682→        let mut current = val;
   683→        for segment in path.split('.') {
   684→            if let Some(idx) = segment.find('[') {
   685→                let key = &segment[..idx];
   686→                let filter_type = &segment[idx + 1..segment.len() - 1];
   687→                let arr = current.get(key)?.as_array()?;
   688→                current = arr
   689→                    .iter()
   690→                    .find(|el| el.get("type").and_then(|t| t.as_str()) == Some(filter_type))?;
   691→            } else {
   692→                current = current.get(segment)?;
   693→            }
   694→        }
   695→        if current.is_null() {
   696→            None
   697→        } else {
   698→            Some(current)
   699→        }
   700→    }
   701→
   702→    /// Verify every Option field in every struct has at least one non-null
   703→    /// occurrence across test data. This catches Option fields that are defined
   704→    /// in a struct but never actually exercised by any test file.
   705→    ///
   706→    /// WARNING: This test uses a static list of Option fields per record type
   707→    /// (see each struct's `optional_fields()` method). It cannot catch Option
   708→    /// fields that are missing from those lists — those fields will silently
   709→    /// remain untested (always None). When adding a new Option field to a
   710→    /// struct, you must also add its camelCase JSON name to the corresponding
   711→    /// `optional_fields()` list.
   712→    #[test]
   713→    fn all_optional_fields_seen() {
   714→        let field_map = Record::optional_fields_by_type();
   715→        let mut seen: HashSet<(String, String)> = HashSet::new();
   716→
   717→        for path in TEST_FILES {
   718→            let file = File::open(path).expect("test file should exist");
   719→            let reader = BufReader::new(file);
   720→            for line in reader.lines() {
   721→                let line = line.unwrap();
   722→                if line.trim().is_empty() {
   723→                    continue;
   724→                }
   725→                let val: Value = match serde_json::from_str(&line) {
   726→                    Ok(v) => v,
   727→                    Err(_) => continue,
   728→                };
   729→                let record_type = match val.get("type").and_then(|t| t.as_str()) {
   730→                    Some(t) => t,
   731→                    None => continue,
   732→                };
   733→                for &(rtype, fields) in &field_map {
   734→                    if rtype != record_type {
   735→                        continue;
   736→                    }
   737→                    for field in fields {
   738→                        if value_at_path(&val, field).is_some() {
   739→                            seen.insert((record_type.to_string(), field.to_string()));
   740→                        }
   741→                    }
   742→                }
   743→            }
   744→        }
   745→
   746→        let mut never_seen = Vec::new();
   747→        for &(rtype, fields) in &field_map {
   748→            for field in fields {
   749→                if !seen.contains(&(rtype.to_string(), field.to_string())) {
   750→                    never_seen.push(format!("{rtype}.{field}"));
   751→                }
   752→            }
   753→        }
   754→        never_seen.sort();
   755→        assert!(
   756→            never_seen.is_empty(),
   757→            "Option fields never seen as Some in test data: {never_seen:?}"
   758→        );
   759→    }
   760→
   761→    #[test]
   762→    fn deserialize_agent_jsonl() {
   763→        assert_eq!(deserialize_file("data/agent-test.jsonl"), 7);
   764→    }
   765→
   766→    #[test]
   767→    fn deserialize_agent_meta() {
   768→        let path = "data/agent-adf742daa2c66fe48.meta.json";
   769→        let file = File::open(path).expect("agent meta file should exist");
   770→        let meta: AgentMeta = serde_json::from_reader(file).expect("agent meta should deserialize");
   771→        assert_eq!(meta.agent_type, "Explore");
   772→        assert!(meta.description.is_some());
   773→    }
   774→
   775→    #[test]
   776→    fn deserialize_agent_meta_no_description() {
   777→        let path = "data/agent-ada8e90bba5770efc.meta.json";
   778→        let file = File::open(path).expect("agent meta file should exist");
   779→        let meta: AgentMeta = serde_json::from_reader(file).expect("agent meta should deserialize");
   780→        assert_eq!(meta.agent_type, "Explore");
   781→        assert!(meta.description.is_none());
   782→    }
   783→
   784→    #[test]
   785→    fn all_variants_covered() {
   786→        let mut seen = HashSet::new();
   787→        for path in TEST_FILES {
   788→            deserialize_file_collecting(path, Some(&mut seen));
   789→        }
   790→        let expected: HashSet<String> =
   791→            Record::all_labels().iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();
   792→        let missing: Vec<_> = expected.difference(&seen).collect();
   793→        assert!(
   794→            missing.is_empty(),
   795→            "Record variants not covered by test data: {missing:?}"
   796→        );
   797→    }
   798→}

for r in user_records:
    tur = r.get('toolUseResult')

    t = tur.get('type', 'NO_TYPE')

content.text:
Here is the complete research report for all 17 fields.

---

## Field Shape Report

Dataset: 1031 records across 6 JSONL files. Breakdown: 92 file-history-snapshot, 314 user, 410 assistant, 180 progress, 23 system, 3 queue-operation, plus a few others.

---

### 1. `trackedFileBackups` (file-history-snapshot → snapshot)

Always present (92/92 records). Always a `dict`. Keys are file paths (strings); values are a consistent struct:

```json
{
  "backupFileName": "5e911ea4c8c6aa59@v1",
  "version": 1,
  "backupTime": "2026-03-23T21:53:23.586Z"
}
```

Most records are empty dicts `{}`. Non-empty ones have the same value shape.

**Consistency:** Consistent. The map value has exactly `backupFileName: String`, `version: u64`, `backupTime: String` (ISO timestamp).

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `HashMap<String, FileBackupEntry>` where `FileBackupEntry` has those three fields.

---

### 2. `toolUseResult` in user records

Present in 229/314 user records. Polymorphic — two top-level shapes:

**Shape A — String** (7 occurrences): Plain error text, e.g. `"Error: Exit code 1\n..."`. No structure.

**Shape B — Dict** (222 occurrences): Multiple variants distinguished by key-signature (no consistent `type` discriminant across all). The `type` field exists in only some variants:

| Key signature | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `stdout, stderr, interrupted, isImage, noOutputExpected` | 103 | Bash tool result |
| `filePath, newString, oldString, originalFile, replaceAll, structuredPatch, userModified` | 35 | Edit tool result |
| `statusChange, success, taskId, updatedFields, verificationNudgeNeeded` | 10 | Task update result |
| `matches, query, total_deferred_tools` | 6 | ToolSearch result |
| `filenames, durationMs, numFiles, truncated` | 5 | Glob result |
| `task` | 5 | Task get result |
| `agentId, content, prompt, status, totalDurationMs, totalTokens, totalToolUseCount, usage` | 3 | Agent result |
| `message` | 2 | Plan mode result |
| `filePath, isAgent, plan` | 2 | Write plan result |
| `content, filenames, mode, numFiles, numLines` | 1 | Grep result |
| `answers, questions` | 1 | Questions tool result |

There are also typed variants: `type: "text"` (file read), `type: "create"`, `type: "update"`.

**Consistency:** Highly polymorphic — 11+ distinct shapes, no single discriminant field.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`). The variants map to whatever tool was called; typing them all would require a large enum.

---

### 3. `todos` in user records

Present in 2/314 user records. Always `[]` (empty list) in all observed data. The list item schema cannot be determined from this dataset.

**Consistency:** Always empty list; item shape unknown.

**Recommendation:** Untyped for now (`Vec<Value>`), or `Vec<TodoItem>` once an item example is found. The outer container is always a list.

---

### 4. `thinkingMetadata` in user records

Present in 1/314 user records. One example:

```json
{
  "level": "high",
  "disabled": false,
  "triggers": []
}
```

Fields: `level: String`, `disabled: bool`, `triggers: Vec<?>` (empty in all observations).

**Consistency:** Only one example, but fields are coherent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable if this structure is confirmed — `ThinkingMetadata { level: String, disabled: bool, triggers: Vec<Value> }`.

---

### 5. `content` in tool_result blocks (structured/list variant)

In user message content blocks, `content` can be a `String` (220 cases) or a `Vec` (9 cases). The list items have type fields:

- `type: "text"` — `{ type, text: String }` (6 seen)
- `type: "tool_reference"` — `{ type, tool_name: String }` (7 seen)

Example list:
```json
[{ "type": "tool_reference", "tool_name": "EnterPlanMode" }]
[{ "type": "text", "text": "..." }]
```

**Consistency:** Two known item variants; both have consistent structure.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — the list variant items are `ContentBlock` (text or tool_reference). The `content` field itself is polymorphic (String | Vec), so the outer `content` field remains `Value` or uses `#[serde(untagged)]` enum.

---

### 6. `input` in tool_use content blocks (assistant records)

Present in 231/231 tool_use blocks. Always a `dict`. The dict shape varies per tool (Read, Bash, Glob, Edit, Agent, ToolSearch, Write, EnterPlanMode, etc.). Per-tool shapes are consistent but differ across tools.

**Consistency:** Always a dict, but the dict keys are tool-specific.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`) — the shape depends on which tool is being called, and there are many tools.

---

### 7. `caller` in tool_use content blocks

Present in 231/231 tool_use blocks (all of them). Always a dict. Only one distinct value observed across the entire dataset:

```json
{"type": "direct"}
```

**Consistency:** Consistent — always `{ "type": "direct" }`.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `CallerInfo { type: String }` or more specifically a unit-like enum `CallerType::Direct`. For now `{ caller_type: String }` is safe.

---

### 8. `container` in assistant message

**Not present** in any of the 410 assistant records in this dataset (0 occurrences). Also not found in progress assistant sub-messages.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Option<Value>`) — shape completely unknown; keep as Value until examples emerge.

---

### 9. `contextManagement` / `context_management` in assistant message

Note: The actual JSON field name is snake_case `context_management` (Anthropic API style), not camelCase.

- **Regular assistant records**: 0 occurrences of `context_management`.
- **Progress agent_progress sub-messages** (assistant type inner messages): 4/41 occurrences.

Only one distinct value observed:
```json
{"applied_edits": []}
```

**Consistency:** Single observed shape, but very sparse.

**Recommendation:** Typeable for now — `ContextManagement { applied_edits: Vec<Value> }`. The `applied_edits` list is always empty in observed data so item type is unknown.

---

### 10. `serverToolUse` / `server_tool_use` in usage objects

Note: The actual JSON field name is snake_case `server_tool_use`.

Present in 246/410 assistant records. Always a dict with exactly two fields:

```json
{
  "web_search_requests": 0,
  "web_fetch_requests": 0
}
```

All 246 examples have the same two keys, both integers (always 0 in this dataset but the field is present).

**Consistency:** Consistent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `ServerToolUse { web_search_requests: u64, web_fetch_requests: u64 }`.

---

### 11. `iterations` in usage objects

Present in 244/410 assistant records. Always a list `[]` (empty) in all observed data. Item shape unknown.

**Consistency:** Always empty; item shape unknown.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Vec<Value>`) until non-empty examples are found.

---

### 12. `message` in progress data

`progress` records don't have a top-level `message` field. The field is inside `progress.data.message` for `agent_progress` records (86/180 progress records).

`data.message` is always a dict with `type` field:
- `type: "user"` (45 occurrences): `{ type, message: { role: "user", content: [...] } }`
- `type: "assistant"` (41 occurrences): `{ type, timestamp, message: { role, content, model, id, type, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage, context_management } }`

**Consistency:** Two known variants with consistent structure.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — discriminated by `type` field. These mirror the existing `UserMessage` and `AssistantMessage` structs with a wrapper.

---

### 13. `prompt` in progress data

Present in 86/180 progress records (all `agent_progress` type). Always a `String`. Contains the agent's prompt text (may be empty string `""`).

**Consistency:** Consistent — always `String`.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `String`.

---

### 14. `normalizedMessages` in progress data

Present in 1/180 progress records. Always `[]` (empty list).

**Consistency:** Only one example, always empty.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Vec<Value>`) — item shape unknown.

---

### 15. `content` in queue-operation records

Present in 2/3 queue-operation records (absent when `operation: "remove"`). Polymorphic:

- Example 1 (real data): `"/effort auto"` — a plain `String`
- Example 2 (test data): `[{ "type": "text", "text": "test content as array" }]` — a list of content blocks

**Consistency:** Polymorphic — String or Vec.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`) — or `#[serde(untagged)]` enum `StringOrBlocks`.

---

### 16. `error` in system records

Present in 1/23 system records (only in `subtype: "api_error"` records). Always a dict:

```json

  "status": 500,
  "headers": {},
  "requestID": null
}
```

Fields: `status: u16`, `headers: Map<String, Value>` (observed empty), `requestID: Option<String>`.

**Consistency:** One example; shape is coherent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `ApiError { status: u16, headers: Map<String, Value>, request_id: Option<String> }`.

---

### 17. `compactMetadata` in system records

Present in 1/23 system records (only in `subtype: "context_pruned"` records). Always a dict:

}
```

content.text:
Here is the complete research report for all 17 fields.

---

## Field Shape Report

Dataset: 1031 records across 6 JSONL files. Breakdown: 92 file-history-snapshot, 314 user, 410 assistant, 180 progress, 23 system, 3 queue-operation, plus a few others.

---

### 1. `trackedFileBackups` (file-history-snapshot → snapshot)

Always present (92/92 records). Always a `dict`. Keys are file paths (strings); values are a consistent struct:

```json
{
  "backupFileName": "5e911ea4c8c6aa59@v1",
  "version": 1,
  "backupTime": "2026-03-23T21:53:23.586Z"
}
```

Most records are empty dicts `{}`. Non-empty ones have the same value shape.

**Consistency:** Consistent. The map value has exactly `backupFileName: String`, `version: u64`, `backupTime: String` (ISO timestamp).

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `HashMap<String, FileBackupEntry>` where `FileBackupEntry` has those three fields.

---

### 2. `toolUseResult` in user records

Present in 229/314 user records. Polymorphic — two top-level shapes:

**Shape A — String** (7 occurrences): Plain error text, e.g. `"Error: Exit code 1\n..."`. No structure.

**Shape B — Dict** (222 occurrences): Multiple variants distinguished by key-signature (no consistent `type` discriminant across all). The `type` field exists in only some variants:

| Key signature | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `stdout, stderr, interrupted, isImage, noOutputExpected` | 103 | Bash tool result |
| `filePath, newString, oldString, originalFile, replaceAll, structuredPatch, userModified` | 35 | Edit tool result |
| `statusChange, success, taskId, updatedFields, verificationNudgeNeeded` | 10 | Task update result |
| `matches, query, total_deferred_tools` | 6 | ToolSearch result |
| `filenames, durationMs, numFiles, truncated` | 5 | Glob result |
| `task` | 5 | Task get result |
| `agentId, content, prompt, status, totalDurationMs, totalTokens, totalToolUseCount, usage` | 3 | Agent result |
| `message` | 2 | Plan mode result |
| `filePath, isAgent, plan` | 2 | Write plan result |
| `content, filenames, mode, numFiles, numLines` | 1 | Grep result |
| `answers, questions` | 1 | Questions tool result |

There are also typed variants: `type: "text"` (file read), `type: "create"`, `type: "update"`.

**Consistency:** Highly polymorphic — 11+ distinct shapes, no single discriminant field.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`). The variants map to whatever tool was called; typing them all would require a large enum.

---

### 3. `todos` in user records

Present in 2/314 user records. Always `[]` (empty list) in all observed data. The list item schema cannot be determined from this dataset.

**Consistency:** Always empty list; item shape unknown.

**Recommendation:** Untyped for now (`Vec<Value>`), or `Vec<TodoItem>` once an item example is found. The outer container is always a list.

---

### 4. `thinkingMetadata` in user records

Present in 1/314 user records. One example:

```json
{
  "level": "high",
  "disabled": false,
  "triggers": []
}
```

Fields: `level: String`, `disabled: bool`, `triggers: Vec<?>` (empty in all observations).

**Consistency:** Only one example, but fields are coherent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable if this structure is confirmed — `ThinkingMetadata { level: String, disabled: bool, triggers: Vec<Value> }`.

---

### 5. `content` in tool_result blocks (structured/list variant)

In user message content blocks, `content` can be a `String` (220 cases) or a `Vec` (9 cases). The list items have type fields:

- `type: "text"` — `{ type, text: String }` (6 seen)
- `type: "tool_reference"` — `{ type, tool_name: String }` (7 seen)

Example list:
```json
[{ "type": "tool_reference", "tool_name": "EnterPlanMode" }]
[{ "type": "text", "text": "..." }]
```

**Consistency:** Two known item variants; both have consistent structure.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — the list variant items are `ContentBlock` (text or tool_reference). The `content` field itself is polymorphic (String | Vec), so the outer `content` field remains `Value` or uses `#[serde(untagged)]` enum.

---

### 6. `input` in tool_use content blocks (assistant records)

Present in 231/231 tool_use blocks. Always a `dict`. The dict shape varies per tool (Read, Bash, Glob, Edit, Agent, ToolSearch, Write, EnterPlanMode, etc.). Per-tool shapes are consistent but differ across tools.

**Consistency:** Always a dict, but the dict keys are tool-specific.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`) — the shape depends on which tool is being called, and there are many tools.

---

### 7. `caller` in tool_use content blocks

Present in 231/231 tool_use blocks (all of them). Always a dict. Only one distinct value observed across the entire dataset:

```json
{"type": "direct"}
```

**Consistency:** Consistent — always `{ "type": "direct" }`.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `CallerInfo { type: String }` or more specifically a unit-like enum `CallerType::Direct`. For now `{ caller_type: String }` is safe.

---

### 8. `container` in assistant message

**Not present** in any of the 410 assistant records in this dataset (0 occurrences). Also not found in progress assistant sub-messages.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Option<Value>`) — shape completely unknown; keep as Value until examples emerge.

---

### 9. `contextManagement` / `context_management` in assistant message

Note: The actual JSON field name is snake_case `context_management` (Anthropic API style), not camelCase.

- **Regular assistant records**: 0 occurrences of `context_management`.
- **Progress agent_progress sub-messages** (assistant type inner messages): 4/41 occurrences.

Only one distinct value observed:
```json
{"applied_edits": []}
```

**Consistency:** Single observed shape, but very sparse.

**Recommendation:** Typeable for now — `ContextManagement { applied_edits: Vec<Value> }`. The `applied_edits` list is always empty in observed data so item type is unknown.

---

### 10. `serverToolUse` / `server_tool_use` in usage objects

Note: The actual JSON field name is snake_case `server_tool_use`.

Present in 246/410 assistant records. Always a dict with exactly two fields:

```json
{
  "web_search_requests": 0,
  "web_fetch_requests": 0
}
```

All 246 examples have the same two keys, both integers (always 0 in this dataset but the field is present).

**Consistency:** Consistent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `ServerToolUse { web_search_requests: u64, web_fetch_requests: u64 }`.

---

### 11. `iterations` in usage objects

Present in 244/410 assistant records. Always a list `[]` (empty) in all observed data. Item shape unknown.

**Consistency:** Always empty; item shape unknown.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Vec<Value>`) until non-empty examples are found.

---

### 12. `message` in progress data

`progress` records don't have a top-level `message` field. The field is inside `progress.data.message` for `agent_progress` records (86/180 progress records).

`data.message` is always a dict with `type` field:
- `type: "user"` (45 occurrences): `{ type, message: { role: "user", content: [...] } }`
- `type: "assistant"` (41 occurrences): `{ type, timestamp, message: { role, content, model, id, type, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage, context_management } }`

**Consistency:** Two known variants with consistent structure.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — discriminated by `type` field. These mirror the existing `UserMessage` and `AssistantMessage` structs with a wrapper.

---

### 13. `prompt` in progress data

Present in 86/180 progress records (all `agent_progress` type). Always a `String`. Contains the agent's prompt text (may be empty string `""`).

**Consistency:** Consistent — always `String`.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `String`.

---

### 14. `normalizedMessages` in progress data

Present in 1/180 progress records. Always `[]` (empty list).

**Consistency:** Only one example, always empty.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Vec<Value>`) — item shape unknown.

---

### 15. `content` in queue-operation records

Present in 2/3 queue-operation records (absent when `operation: "remove"`). Polymorphic:

- Example 1 (real data): `"/effort auto"` — a plain `String`
- Example 2 (test data): `[{ "type": "text", "text": "test content as array" }]` — a list of content blocks

**Consistency:** Polymorphic — String or Vec.

**Recommendation:** Untyped (`Value`) — or `#[serde(untagged)]` enum `StringOrBlocks`.

---

### 16. `error` in system records

Present in 1/23 system records (only in `subtype: "api_error"` records). Always a dict:

```json
{
  "status": 500,
  "headers": {},
  "requestID": null
}
```

Fields: `status: u16`, `headers: Map<String, Value>` (observed empty), `requestID: Option<String>`.

**Consistency:** One example; shape is coherent.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `ApiError { status: u16, headers: Map<String, Value>, request_id: Option<String> }`.

---

### 17. `compactMetadata` in system records

Present in 1/23 system records (only in `subtype: "context_pruned"` records). Always a dict:

```json
{
  "removedMessages": 5
}
```

**Consistency:** One example; single field.

**Recommendation:** Typeable — `CompactMetadata { removed_messages: u64 }` (with `#[serde(rename = "removedMessages")]`).

---

## Summary Table

| # | Field | Location | Shape | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `trackedFileBackups` | snapshot | `HashMap<String, FileBackupEntry>` — consistent struct values | **Typeable** |
| 2 | `toolUseResult` | user | String or 11+ distinct dict shapes | **Untyped** |
| 3 | `todos` | user | `Vec<?>` always empty | **Vec\<Value\>** (unknown items) |
| 4 | `thinkingMetadata` | user | `{ level, disabled, triggers }` — one example | **Typeable** |
| 5 | `content` (tool_result list) | user message | `Vec<{type:text|tool_reference,...}>` | **Typeable** items |
| 6 | `input` | tool_use | Dict, tool-specific shape | **Untyped** |
| 7 | `caller` | tool_use | Always `{"type":"direct"}` | **Typeable** |
| 8 | `container` | assistant.message | Not observed (0 examples) | **Untyped** |
| 9 | `context_management` | assistant.message (via progress) | `{"applied_edits":[]}` — sparse | **Typeable** (tentative) |
| 10 | `server_tool_use` | usage | `{web_search_requests, web_fetch_requests}` — consistent | **Typeable** |
| 11 | `iterations` | usage | `Vec<?>` always empty | **Vec\<Value\>** (unknown items) |
| 12 | `message` | progress.data | Dict with `type: user|assistant` wrapper | **Typeable** |
| 13 | `prompt` | progress.data | Always `String` | **Typeable** |
| 14 | `normalizedMessages` | progress.data | `Vec<?>` always empty | **Vec\<Value\>** (unknown items) |
| 15 | `content` | queue-operation | String or Vec — polymorphic | **Untyped** |
| 16 | `error` | system | `{status, headers, requestID}` — one example | **Typeable** |
| 17 | `compactMetadata` | system | `{removedMessages: u64}` — one example | **Typeable** |

Great research. Here's what I'm planning for 0.13.0:

**New `Untyped` newtype** — wraps `serde_json::Value`

**Replace with real typed structs:**
| Field | New type |
|---|---|
| `tracked_file_backups` | `HashMap<String, FileBackupEntry>` (many examples) |
| `server_tool_use` | `ServerToolUse` (246 examples) |
| `caller` | `Caller` (231 examples) |
| `prompt` (ProgressData) | `String` (86 examples) |
| `compact_metadata` | `CompactMetadata` (1 example) |
| `thinking_metadata` | `ThinkingMetadata` (1 example) |
| `error` (SystemRecord) | `ApiError` (1 example) |

**Replace with `Untyped`:**
| Field | Why |
|---|---|
| `tool_use_result` | 11+ distinct shapes |
| `todos` | always empty, unknown items |
| `input` (tool_use) | tool-specific |
| `container` | never observed |
| `context_management` | sparse, complex |
| `iterations` | always empty |
| `message` (ProgressData) | complex nested |
| `normalized_messages` | always empty |
| `content` (queue-op) | polymorphic |
| `ToolResultContent::Structured` | fallback variant |

The few-example typed structs (1 example) are intentional — `deny_unknown_fields` will catch any upstream schema additions, and we add `Option` fields as needed. That's better than silently ignoring unknown data.

## Replace serde_json::Value with typed structs

  Several struct fields use `serde_json::Value` as a catch-all for
  JSON we haven't fully typed yet. This works but loses type safety
  and doesn't catch schema changes via `deny_unknown_fields`.

  Fields currently using Value:
  - `UserRecord`: thinkingMetadata, toolUseResult, todos
  - `SystemRecord`: error, compactMetadata
  - `ProgressData`: message, prompt, normalizedMessages,
    serverToolUse, iterations
  - `QueueOperationRecord`: content
  - `AssistantRecord` nested: serverToolUse, iterations,
    caller, container, contextManagement

  Options:
  1. Typed structs per field — best safety, most work, risks
     breakage on upstream schema changes
  2. Custom `Opaque(Value)` newtype — signals "structured but
     not yet typed", distinguishes from deliberately modeled
     fields, can incrementally promote to real structs

  If we go with a catch-all, prefer our own type over bare
  `serde_json::Value` so grep can find all untyped fields and
  we can add validation or logging in one place later.

input.old_string:
## Replace serde_json::Value with typed structs

  Several struct fields use `serde_json::Value` as a catch-all for
  JSON we haven't fully typed yet. This works but loses type safety
  and doesn't catch schema changes via `deny_unknown_fields`.

  Fields currently using Value:
  - `UserRecord`: thinkingMetadata, toolUseResult, todos
  - `SystemRecord`: error, compactMetadata
  - `ProgressData`: message, prompt, normalizedMessages,
    serverToolUse, iterations
  - `QueueOperationRecord`: content
  - `AssistantRecord` nested: serverToolUse, iterations,
    caller, container, contextManagement

  Options:
  1. Typed structs per field — best safety, most work, risks
     breakage on upstream schema changes
  2. Custom `Opaque(Value)` newtype — signals "structured but
     not yet typed", distinguishes from deliberately modeled
     fields, can incrementally promote to real structs

  If we go with a catch-all, prefer our own type over bare
  `serde_json::Value` so grep can find all untyped fields and
  we can add validation or logging in one place later.
input.new_string:
## Replace serde_json::Value with typed structs (20260326 0.13.0)

  Replaced all bare `serde_json::Value` fields with either typed
  structs or the new `Untyped(Value)` newtype. `Value` is the only
  external library type that was leaking into the domain model —
  `Untyped` keeps the serde dependency internal and makes it easy
  to grep for remaining untyped fields.

  New typed structs:
  - `FileBackupEntry` — trackedFileBackups map values
  - `ServerToolUse` — web_search_requests, web_fetch_requests
  - `Caller` — always `{"type":"direct"}`
  - `ThinkingMetadata` — level, disabled, triggers
  - `CompactMetadata` — removedMessages
  - `ApiError` — status, headers, requestID

  Fields changed to `String` (was `Value` but always a string):
  - `ProgressData.prompt`

  Fields changed to `Untyped` (genuinely polymorphic or unknown):
  - `UserRecord`: toolUseResult, todos
  - `AssistantMessage`: container, contextManagement
  - `AssistantContentBlock::ToolUse`: input
  - `Usage`: iterations
  - `ProgressData`: message, normalizedMessages
  - `QueueOperationRecord`: content
  - `ToolResultContent::Structured`

  Discovery during implementation: `FileBackupEntry.backupFileName`
  can be null — made it `Option<String>`.
